Q Text: Given the following relational algebra query "Who wrote the Track 'Californication'?:  σWrites.artist_id=Artist.artist_id(σTrack.track_id=Writes.track_id(σTrack.track_title='Californication'(Track x Writes x Artist))) Give an equivalent SQL query:
Answer: SELECT  name FROM Track, Writes, Artist WHERE Track.track_title='Californication'  AND Track.track_id=Writes.track_id AND Writes.artist_id=Artist.artist_id;

Q Text: Given the following relational algebra query:      σduration>300(Track) ⨝ Performs ⨝ σname='Queen'(Artist) Give an equivalent SQL query:
Answer: SELECT Track.track_title FROM Track JOIN Performs ON Track.track_id = Performs.track_id JOIN Artist ON Performs.artist_id = Artist.artist_id WHERE Track.duration > 300   AND Artist.name = 'Queen';

Q Text: The following relational algebra query uses a self-join and renaming to find each track that is on the same album as the track titled 'Overpass Graffiti':    πtrack_title(Track ⨝album_id=album_id2 ρalbum_id2(πalbum_id(σtrack_title='Overpass Graffiti'(Track)))) Give an equivalent SQL query.
Answer: SELECT Track.track_title FROM Track JOIN (SELECT Track.album_id AS album_id2       FROM Track       WHERE Track.track_title = 'Overpass Graffiti') AS album ON Track.album_id = album.album_id2;

Q Text: Given the following relational algebra query:      σduration>300(Track) ⨝ Performs ⨝ σname='Queen'(Artist) Give an equivalent SQL query:

Q Text: The following relational algebra query uses a self-join and renaming to find each track that is on the same album as the track titled 'Overpass Graffiti':    πtrack_title(Track ⨝album_id=album_id2 ρalbum_id2(πalbum_id(σtrack_title='Overpass Graffiti'(Track)))) Give an equivalent SQL query.
Answer: -- Select the track titles of tracks on the same album SELECT t1.track_title  -- Select the track titles of tracks on the same album   -- From the Track table (renamed as t1) FROM Track AS t1     -- Join the Track table again (renamed as t2) on the album_id JOIN Track AS t2 ON t1.album_id = t2.album_id     -- Filter the tracks where one of them is 'Overpass Graffiti' WHERE t2.track_title = 'Overpass Graffiti';

Q Text: Given the following relational algebra query "Who wrote the Track 'Californication'?:  σWrites.artist_id=Artist.artist_id(σTrack.track_id=Writes.track_id(σTrack.track_title='Californication'(Track x Writes x Artist))) Give an equivalent SQL query:
Answer: SELECT Artist.name FROM Track JOIN Writes ON Track.track_id = Writes.track_id JOIN Artist ON Writes.artist_id = Artist.artist_id WHERE Track.track_title = 'Californication';

Q Text: Given the following relational algebra query:      σduration>300(Track) ⨝ Performs ⨝ σname='Queen'(Artist) Give an equivalent SQL query:
Answer: SELECT track.* FROM Track JOIN Performs ON Track.track_id = Performs.track_id JOIN Artist ON Performs.artist_id = Artist.artist_id WHERE Track.duration > 300 AND Artist.name = 'Queen';

Q Text: The following relational algebra query uses a self-join and renaming to find each track that is on the same album as the track titled 'Overpass Graffiti':    πtrack_title(Track ⨝album_id=album_id2 ρalbum_id2(πalbum_id(σtrack_title='Overpass Graffiti'(Track)))) Give an equivalent SQL query.
Answer: SELECT T1.track_title FROM Track AS T1 JOIN ( SELECT album_id FROM Track WHERE track_title = 'Overpass Graffiti' )  AS T2 ON T1.album_id = T2.album_id;

Q Text: Given the following relational algebra query "names of Artists that Produced a Track":     πname(Artist ⨝ Produces) Give an equivalent SQL query:
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT  name FROM Artist NATURAL JOIN Produces;

Q Text: Given the following relational algebra query "Who wrote the Track 'Californication'?:  σWrites.artist_id=Artist.artist_id(σTrack.track_id=Writes.track_id(σTrack.track_title='Californication'(Track x Writes x Artist))) Give an equivalent SQL query:
Answer: SELECT Artist.name, Writes.role FROM Track,Writes,Artist WHERE (Writes.artist_id = Artist.artist_id) AND (Track.track_id = Writes.track_id) AND (Track.track_title = 'Californication');

Q Text: Given the following relational algebra query:      σduration>300(Track) ⨝ Performs ⨝ σname='Queen'(Artist) Give an equivalent SQL query:
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Track.* FROM Track NATURAL JOIN Performs NATURAL JOIN Artist WHERE Track.duration > 300 AND Artist.name = 'Queen';

Q Text: The following relational algebra query uses a self-join and renaming to find each track that is on the same album as the track titled 'Overpass Graffiti':    πtrack_title(Track ⨝album_id=album_id2 ρalbum_id2(πalbum_id(σtrack_title='Overpass Graffiti'(Track)))) Give an equivalent SQL query.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT T1.track_title FROM Track T1 JOIN Track T2 ON T1.album_id = T2.album_id WHERE T2.track_title = 'Overpass Graffiti';
